items.title: AMOS Stealer delivered via Cursor AI agent session
items.description: On April 23, 2026, Field Effect MDR identified AMOS Stealer malware delivered through a novel technique exploiting Cursor AI agent sessions running Claude Code. The attack employed social engineering to manipulate operators into prompting the AI agent to download and execute malicious AppleScript loaders. The heavily obfuscated scripts performed sandbox evasion checks, collected sensitive data inc
items.ai_summary: The AMOS Stealer malware is delivered through a novel technique exploiting Cursor AI agent sessions running Claude Code, leveraging social engineering to manipulate operators into prompting the AI agent to download and execute malicious AppleScript loaders. The attack utilizes heavily obfuscated scripts to perform sandbox evasion checks and collect sensitive data, with TTPs including T1059.002 for AppleScript execution, T1204 for user execution, and T1027 for obfuscation. Real-world impact includes the potential for significant data breaches and authentication certificate theft, affecting organizations that rely on Cursor AI agent sessions. To remediate, defenders should update their Cursor AI agent sessions to the latest version, ensure that all AppleScript loaders are vetted and approved, and implement robust sandbox evasion detection mechanisms. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to T1562 and T1649, and ensure that all authentication certificates are properly secured and monitored.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1562| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cloud Atlas activity in the second half of 2025 and early 2026: new tools and a new payload
items.description: Cloud Atlas APT group targeted government organizations and commercial companies in Russia and Belarus during late 2025 and early 2026, employing phishing campaigns with malicious ZIP archives containing LNK shortcuts. The attackers deployed multiple backdoors including VBCloud for file theft and PowerShower for network reconnaissance. New tools identified include PowerCloud, which exfiltrates dat
items.ai_summary: The Cloud Atlas APT group has been targeting government organizations and commercial companies in Russia and Belarus through phishing campaigns, utilizing malicious ZIP archives containing LNK shortcuts to deploy multiple backdoors, including VBCloud for file theft and PowerShower for network reconnaissance. The attackers have also been using new tools such as PowerCloud for data exfiltration. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to significant data breaches and network compromise, affecting sensitive information and disrupting operations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and conduct regular network monitoring for suspicious activity. Specifically, defenders should block all emails with malicious ZIP archives and LNK shortcuts, and restrict access to sensitive data and systems.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1560.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Tracking Iranian APT Screening Serpens’ 2026 Espionage Campaigns
items.description: Unit 42 researchers identified six new remote access Trojan variants deployed by Iran-nexus APT group Screening Serpens between February and April 2026, coinciding with a regional conflict starting February 28, 2026. The group targeted entities in the U.S., Israel, UAE, and other Middle Eastern locations, primarily focusing on technology sector professionals through highly tailored social engineer
items.ai_summary: The Screening Serpens APT group has been identified as deploying six new remote access Trojan variants between February and April 2026, targeting technology sector professionals in the U.S., Israel, UAE, and other Middle Eastern locations. The group's primary attack vector involves highly tailored social engineering tactics to gain initial access, leveraging T1219 (Remote Access Software) to establish a foothold. If exploited, the malware allows the threat actors to remotely access and control compromised systems, potentially leading to data breaches, intellectual property theft, and other malicious activities. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering and employee education programs to prevent social engineering attacks, and ensure all remote access software is up-to-date and configured with strong authentication and access controls. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious network activity and implement a zero-trust security model to limit the spread of potential malware.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Fast and Furious - Nimbus Manticore Operations During the Iranian Conflict
items.description: The Iranian IRGC-affiliated threat actor Nimbus Manticore launched sophisticated cyber operations during Operation Epic Fury, the US military campaign against Iran beginning February 28, 2026. The campaigns targeted organizations in aviation and software sectors across the United States, Europe, and Middle East using career-themed phishing lures. For the first time, the actor employed SEO poisonin
items.ai_summary: The Nimbus Manticore threat actor, affiliated with the Iranian IRGC, has launched targeted cyber operations against organizations in the aviation and software sectors across the United States, Europe, and Middle East. The campaigns utilize career-themed phishing lures, as well as SEO poisoning for the first time, to gain initial access to victim systems. If exploited, these attacks can lead to significant data breaches and disruption of critical services, with potential real-world impact including compromised flight safety and intellectual property theft. To remediate these threats, defenders should implement robust email filtering and user education programs, as well as monitor for suspicious search engine optimization activity. Additionally, organizations should ensure all employee-facing systems are updated with the latest security patches and configure web application firewalls to detect and prevent malicious traffic.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1608.006| C[Persistence]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1055| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: From edge appliance to enterprise compromise: Multi-stage Linux intrusion via F5 and Confluence
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage intrusion began with the compromise of an internet-facing F5 BIG-IP load balancer running an end-of-life version. The threat actor established SSH access to a Linux server using privileged credentials, then conducted extensive reconnaissance including network scanning with Nmap and service enumeration with gowitness. Following horizontal and vertical scanning operations
items.ai_summary: The threat actor exploited an end-of-life F5 BIG-IP load balancer to gain initial access, then used privileged credentials to establish SSH access to a Linux server. The actor conducted extensive reconnaissance, including network scanning with Nmap and service enumeration with gowitness, to gather information about the target network. This multi-stage intrusion allowed the actor to move laterally and vertically, potentially compromising sensitive data and systems. To remediate, defenders should immediately upgrade any end-of-life F5 BIG-IP load balancers to a supported version, such as BIG-IP 17.0.0 or later, and rotate all privileged credentials. Additionally, defenders should implement robust network segmentation and monitoring to detect and prevent similar intrusions.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1595| B[Reconnaissance]:::tactic
    B -->|T1046| C[Resource Development]:::tactic
    C -->|T1213| D[Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1104| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Laravel Lang Compromised with RCE Backdoor Across 700+ Versions
items.description: Community-maintained Laravel Lang packages were compromised with remote code execution backdoors affecting over 700 versions across multiple repositories including laravel-lang/lang, laravel-lang/http-statuses, laravel-lang/attributes, and laravel-lang/actions. The attack involved coordinated rapid tag publishing on May 22-23, 2026, suggesting organization-level credential compromise. A malicious
items.ai_summary: The Laravel Lang package compromise involves a remote code execution (RCE) backdoor affecting over 700 versions across multiple repositories. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications (T1190) through coordinated rapid tag publishing, suggesting organization-level credential compromise. If exploited, this vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities, affecting developers and organizations using the compromised packages. To remediate, developers should immediately remove the compromised packages and update to a secure version, such as the newly released versions of laravel-lang/lang, laravel-lang/http-statuses, laravel-lang/attributes, and laravel-lang/actions, and monitor their applications for suspicious activity. Additionally, developers should review their credential management and access controls to prevent similar compromises in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Android Trojan Abuses Commercial Rooting Tool and Steals Private Information
items.description: Rootnik is an Android trojan that exploits vulnerabilities in Android 4.3 and earlier by weaponizing a Chinese commercial rooting tool called Root Assistant. The malicious operation spreads through repackaged legitimate applications distributed globally, affecting users primarily in the United States, Malaysia, Thailand, Lebanon and Taiwan. After installation, Rootnik gains root access using stole
items.ai_summary: The Rootnik Android trojan exploits vulnerabilities in Android 4.3 and earlier versions by utilizing a commercial rooting tool called Root Assistant, allowing it to gain root access and steal private information. The trojan spreads through repackaged legitimate applications, primarily affecting users in the United States, Malaysia, Thailand, Lebanon, and Taiwan. To exploit this vulnerability, attackers use the T1068 tactic for privilege escalation, enabling them to access sensitive data. Defenders can remediate this threat by ensuring their Android devices are updated to versions later than 4.3, avoiding the installation of repackaged applications, and implementing robust security measures such as mobile device management and regular security audits. Additionally, users should be cautious when granting root access to any application and monitor their device for suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1068| B[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    B -->|T1210| C[Exploitation of Remote Services]:::tactic
    C -->|T1082| D[System Information Discovery]:::tactic
    D -->|T1539| E[Steal Private Information]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState Deserialization Vulnerability
items.description: In late 2025, an unknown threat actor exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System widely used in Japan. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5426, allowed unauthenticated remote code execution through ViewState deserialization attacks. The issue stemmed from identical hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys distributed across multiple customer deployments
items.ai_summary: The exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState deserialization vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5426, is a critical zero-day vulnerability that allows unauthenticated remote code execution. This vulnerability affects multiple customer deployments of KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System widely used in Japan, due to identical hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to a full system compromise, allowing threat actors to execute arbitrary code, steal sensitive data, and disrupt the learning management system. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately update their KnowledgeDeliver deployments to the latest version, which includes a unique ASP.NET machine key for each customer, and restrict access to the system to only trusted IP addresses. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1210| D[Exploitation for Client Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Token Bingo: Don't Let Your Code be the Winner
items.description: In early April 2026, a large-scale device code phishing campaign targeted organizations across multiple sectors and regions, exploiting OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant. Threat actors leveraged the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform, originating primarily from IP address 216.203.20[.]95. The campaign used high-fidelity lures directing victims to Microsoft's legitimate device login flow, wh
items.ai_summary: The Token Bingo phishing campaign, detected in early April 2026, exploits the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant, targeting organizations across multiple sectors and regions. This campaign leverages the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform, primarily originating from IP address 216.203.20[.]95, and uses high-fidelity lures to direct victims to Microsoft's legitimate device login flow. If exploited, this campaign can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information and systems, affecting various organizations and individuals who use Microsoft services. To remediate, defenders should implement robust phishing detection and prevention measures, such as multi-factor authentication and user education, and monitor for suspicious activity originating from the identified IP address. Additionally, organizations should review their OAuth 2.0 implementation and ensure that it follows best practices to prevent similar exploits.

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold

items.title: RemotePE: The Lazarus RAT that lives in memory
items.description: A sophisticated memory-only toolset used by a North Korean Lazarus subgroup targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations consists of three malware families forming a chain. DPAPILoader decrypts and loads RemotePELoader from disk using Windows Data Protection API. RemotePELoader beacons to command-and-control servers and retrieves RemotePE, a fully-fledged remote access trojan executed enti
items.ai_summary: The RemotePE threat involves a sophisticated memory-only toolset used by the North Korean Lazarus subgroup, targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations. This toolset consists of three malware families forming a chain, starting with DPAPILoader, which decrypts and loads RemotePELoader from disk using the Windows Data Protection API. RemotePELoader then beacons to command-and-control servers and retrieves RemotePE, a fully-fledged remote access trojan executed entirely in memory. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement network traffic monitoring to detect unusual application layer protocol activity, as indicated by T1071, and restrict remote access software, as indicated by T1219, by configuring firewalls to block incoming connections on unused ports and regularly reviewing system logs for signs of unauthorized access. Additionally, defenders should prioritize securing sensitive data and implementing robust access controls to mitigate the impact of potential exploitation.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2020-37235: WordPress Theme Wibar 1.1.8 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability...
items.description: WordPress Theme Wibar 1.1.8 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the Brand component that allows authenticated users to inject malicious scripts by manipulating the Logo URL parameter. Attackers with editor, administrator, contributor, or author privileges can inject base64-encoded script payloads through the ftc_brand_url input field to execute arbitrary JavaScript when users v
items.ai_summary: CVE-2020-37235 is a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the WordPress Theme Wibar version 1.1.8, specifically in the Brand component. This vulnerability allows authenticated users with editor, administrator, contributor, or author privileges to inject malicious scripts by manipulating the Logo URL parameter. The attack vector involves injecting base64-encoded script payloads through the ftc_brand_url input field, which can execute arbitrary JavaScript when users visit the compromised page. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to a patched version of the Wibar theme, ensuring that the Brand component is updated to prevent malicious script injection. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by restricting access to the ftc_brand_url input field and validating user input to prevent arbitrary JavaScript execution.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1060| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1047| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2020-37234: Internet Download Manager 6.38.12 contains a buffer overflow vulnerability in th...
items.description: Internet Download Manager 6.38.12 contains a buffer overflow vulnerability in the Scheduler component that allows local attackers to crash the application by supplying oversized input. Attackers can paste malicious data exceeding 5000 bytes into the 'Open the following file when done' field to trigger a denial of service condition.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2020-37234 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in Internet Download Manager 6.38.12's Scheduler component, allowing local attackers to crash the application by supplying oversized input exceeding 5000 bytes into the 'Open the following file when done' field, resulting in a denial of service condition. This vulnerability can be exploited by attackers to disrupt the normal functioning of the application, affecting users who rely on it for downloading files. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to Internet Download Manager version 6.38.13 or later, which includes a patch for this issue. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by being cautious when pasting data into the 'Open the following file when done' field and avoiding oversized input. Threat actors may utilize this vulnerability as part of a larger campaign to disrupt user activity, but no specific attribution has been mentioned.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1498| C[Network Denial of Service]:::tactic

    D -->|T1498| E[Denial of Service]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2020-37233: WordPress Plugin Buddypress 6.2.0 contains a persistent cross-site scripting vul...
items.description: WordPress Plugin Buddypress 6.2.0 contains a persistent cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers with moderator privileges to inject malicious script code through the figure parameter in wp:html blocks. Attackers can inject iframe elements with event handlers like onload that execute when administrators or privileged users preview or view the affected page content, en
items.ai_summary: CVE-2020-37233 is a persistent cross-site scripting vulnerability in WordPress Plugin Buddypress version 6.2.0, allowing authenticated attackers with moderator privileges to inject malicious script code through the figure parameter in wp:html blocks. The vulnerability can be exploited by injecting iframe elements with event handlers like onload, which execute when administrators or privileged users preview or view the affected page content. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update the Buddypress plugin to version 6.2.1 or later, and restrict moderator privileges to trusted users only. Additionally, defenders can configure their web application firewalls to detect and prevent malicious script injections. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and lateral movement within the network.
items.workflow_graph:

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2020-37232: Advanced System Care Service 13.0.0.157 contains an unquoted service path vulner...
items.description: Advanced System Care Service 13.0.0.157 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in the AdvancedSystemCareService13 service binary path that allows local attackers to escalate privileges. Attackers can place malicious executables in the system root path that will be executed with LocalSystem privileges during service startup or system reboot.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2020-37232 is a privilege escalation vulnerability in Advanced System Care Service 13.0.0.157, where an unquoted service path in the AdvancedSystemCareService13 service binary path allows local attackers to execute malicious executables with LocalSystem privileges. This vulnerability can be exploited by placing malicious files in the system root path, which will be executed during service startup or system reboot, resulting in privilege escalation. The real-world impact of this vulnerability includes potential data breaches and system compromise, affecting users of Advanced System Care Service 13.0.0.157. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to a patched version of Advanced System Care Service, such as version 13.0.0.158 or later, and ensure that all service paths are properly quoted to prevent similar vulnerabilities. Additionally, defenders should monitor system logs for suspicious activity and implement security controls to restrict access to the system root path.

items.title: CVE-2020-37231: Privacy Drive 3.17.0 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in the pdsv...
items.description: Privacy Drive 3.17.0 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in the pdsvc.exe service binary that allows local attackers to escalate privileges by exploiting the service startup process. Attackers can place malicious executables in the unquoted path directories to execute arbitrary code with LocalSystem privileges during service startup or system reboot.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2020-37231 is a vulnerability in Privacy Drive 3.17.0 that allows local attackers to escalate privileges by exploiting an unquoted service path in the pdsvc.exe service binary. This vulnerability can be exploited by placing malicious executables in the unquoted path directories, which can then be executed with LocalSystem privileges during service startup or system reboot. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is that attackers can gain elevated access to sensitive systems and data, potentially leading to data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to Privacy Drive version 3.18.0 or later, and ensure that all service paths are properly quoted to prevent exploitation. Additionally, defenders should monitor system logs for suspicious activity and implement least privilege principles to limit the damage in case of an exploit.

items.description: Syncplify.me Server! 5.0.37 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in the SMWebRestServicev5 service that allows local attackers to escalate privileges by exploiting the unquoted binary path. Attackers can insert a malicious executable into the service path and execute it with LocalSystem privileges when the service restarts or the system reboots.

items.title: CVE-2020-37229: OKI sPSV Port Manager 1.0.41 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in ...
items.description: OKI sPSV Port Manager 1.0.41 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in the sPSVOpLclSrv service that allows local attackers to escalate privileges by inserting executable files into the unquoted path. Attackers can place a malicious executable in a directory within the service path that will execute with LocalSystem privileges when the service restarts or the system reboots.

items.title: CVE-2020-37228: iDS6 DSSPro Digital Signage System 6.2 contains a CAPTCHA security bypass vulner...
items.description: iDS6 DSSPro Digital Signage System 6.2 contains a CAPTCHA security bypass vulnerability that allows attackers to bypass authentication by requesting the autoLoginVerifyCode object. Attackers can retrieve valid CAPTCHA codes via the login endpoint and use them to perform brute-force attacks against user accounts.

items.title: CVE-2020-37227: HS Brand Logo Slider 2.1 contains an unrestricted file upload vulnerability that...
items.description: HS Brand Logo Slider 2.1 contains an unrestricted file upload vulnerability that allows authenticated users to bypass client-side file extension validation by uploading arbitrary files. Attackers can intercept upload requests to the logoupload parameter in the admin interface and rename files to executable extensions .php to achieve remote code execution.

items.title:
CVE-2026-46719: Net::Statsd::Lite versions before 0.9.0 for Perl allowed metric injections.

The...
items.description:
Net::Statsd::Lite versions before 0.9.0 for Perl allowed metric injections.

The metric names were not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics.

items.description: Notable integrations include CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, Microsoft, Okta, Zscaler, Netskope, Cloudflare, Fortinet, and Wiz. The post Anthropic Expands Claude’s Enterprise Security Governance With 28 New Integrations appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Hackers Exploited KnowledgeDeliver Zero-Day for Web Shell Deployment
items.description: Hardcoded machineKey values in a configuration file enabled ViewState deserialization attacks leading to remote code execution. The post Hackers Exploited KnowledgeDeliver Zero-Day for Web Shell Deployment appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Watch on Demand: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit – All Sessions Available
items.description: Register to enjoy free access and explore the tools, strategies, and frameworks needed to build a resilient security program for a world where every minute counts. The post Watch on Demand: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit – All Sessions Available appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Open Source DockSec Uses AI to Cut Through Vulnerability Noise in Docker Images
items.description: DockSec, an OWASP incubator project, correlates findings from multiple container security scanners and uses AI to generate plain-English remediation guidance and exact Dockerfile fixes. The post Open Source DockSec Uses AI to Cut Through Vulnerability Noise in Docker Images appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
items.description: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) was supposed to close a critical gap in identity security. It meant that, even if an attacker possessed the account credentials, they couldn't log in without the second factor. While that logic was sound, attackers have now figured out that they don't need to steal the second factor: they just need the user to hand it over. If your workforce authenticates with

items.title: Lithuania Suspects Foreign Involvement in Data Leak of Over 600,000 National Register Entries
items.description: Lithuanian authorities are on high alert after a massive data leak involving more than 600,000 entries from national data registers. The post Lithuania Suspects Foreign Involvement in Data Leak of Over 600,000 National Register Entries appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Admins of Bulletproof Hosting Service Used by Russian Hackers Arrested in Netherlands
items.description: The two own Dutch companies that allegedly provided bulletproof hosting services to Russia-aligned threat actors. The post Admins of Bulletproof Hosting Service Used by Russian Hackers Arrested in Netherlands appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CERT-In Recommends 12-Hour Patching for Internet-Facing Flaws Amid AI-Assisted Attacks
items.description: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued new guidelines requiring organizations to patch critical security vulnerabilities in internet-exposed systems within 12 hours of being flagged where "feasible" to safeguard against potential threats stemming from threat actors' abuse of artificial intelligence (AI) tools and large language models (LLMs) to automate vulnerability

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Drupal vulnerability
items.description: CISA has given U.S. government agencies until Wednesday evening to secure their servers against an SQL injection vulnerability in the Drupal content management system (CMS) that it flagged as actively exploited. [...]

items.title: Microsoft: Domain Controller lookup may fail on Windows Server 2016
items.description: Microsoft has confirmed a new known issue affecting Windows Server 2016 systems that causes domain controller lookups to fail after installing the KB5087537 May 2026 security update. [...]

items.title: Iranian Hackers Deploy MiniFast and MiniJunk V2 via Phishing and SEO Poisoning
items.description: The Iranian state-sponsored threat actor known as Nimbus Manticore (aka Screening Serpens and UNC1549) has been attributed to a fresh campaign using lures impersonating organizations in the aviation and software sectors across the U.S., Europe, and the Middle East following the joint U.S.-Israeli military campaign against the country in late February 2026. The activity, besides embracing

items.title: 7-Eleven data breach exposes personal information of 185,000 people
items.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole the personal information of over 183,000 people after hacking the systems of convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven in April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

items.title: KnowledgeDeliver LMS Flaw Exploited to Deploy Godzilla and Cobalt Strike
items.description: A now-patched high-severity security flaw affecting Digital Knowledge KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System (LMS) popular in Japan, was exploited as a zero-day to deliver the Godzilla web shell and ultimately facilitate the deployment of Cobalt Strike Beacon. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5426 (CVSS score: 7.5), stems from the use of hard-coded ASP.NET machine keys, leading to

items.title: Kremlin appoints cyber executive with alleged GRU ties to Security Council role
items.description: Andrei Kozlov, the former head of a cybersecurity center within Russia’s state-owned defense conglomerate Rostec, was named an aide to Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu on Friday.

items.title: Anthropic’s restricted Claude Mythos model may be coming to Claude Code
items.description: Anthropic appears to be preparing for the public rollout of the Mythos model, which was announced in April as a restricted model that poses major security risks to private and public software. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Access VBA, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: Microsoft Access files (Microsoft Office&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Database) can contain VBA code.

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Flaws, Defender 0-Days, Router Botnets, and Supply Chain Chaos
items.description: Monday recap. Same mess, new week. A sketchy dev tool got people pwned, old bugs came back from the dead, and security products somehow needed protecting from themselves. A bunch of companies spent the week checking old boxes and forgotten servers they should've patched years ago. Good times. Phishing crews are getting smarter too - less obvious scam junk, more targeted stuff that actually

items.title: Ghost CMS Vulnerability Exploited to Hack Over 700 Websites
items.description: Sites belonging to major universities such as Harvard and Oxford, as well as DuckDuckGo, have been compromised in the attack. The post Ghost CMS Vulnerability Exploited to Hack Over 700 Websites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-24, (Mon, May 25th)
items.description: TeamPCP now operates across three package ecosystems in parallel, it reached GitHub&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s own internal codebase, it trojanized an officially Microsoft-published Python SDK, and it appears to have open-sourced its own framework on GitHub.

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
items.description: The FBI is warning about the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform (PhaaS) that is used to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts by abusing OAuth device code authentication to steal session tokens and bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA). [...]

items.title: Oncology Institute Discloses Data Breach
items.description: The affected third-party vendor has not been named, but one possible candidate is TriZetto. The post Oncology Institute Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
items.description: Threat actors are exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code with an aim to fuel ClickFix attacks. According to QiAnXin XLab, the activity involves the exploitation of CVE-2026-26980 (CVSS score: 9.4), an SQL injection vulnerability in Ghost's Content API that could allow an unauthenticated attacker to read arbitrary data from the

items.title: The Alert Firehose Finally Meets Its Match
items.description: Ask a cybersecurity pro about Network Detection and Response (NDR) and you might still hear "Noisy," "Too much data." But ask the teams running NDR that includes agentic AI capabilities and you'll hear they're actually using it to catch threats earlier, triage faster, and chase fewer false positives. The old complaint lingers in part because reputations are sticky, and because NDR has evolved

items.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a cross-platform malware called RemotePE that has been put to use by the North Korea-linked Lazarus Group in attacks targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations. RemotePE, per NCC Group subsidiary Fox-IT, is part of a multi-stage attack chain that involves two loaders tracked as DPAPILoader and RemotePELoader. "DPAPILoader decrypts and

items.title: Wireshark 4.6.6 Released, (Sun, May 24th)
items.description: Wireshark release 4.6.6 fixes 1 vulnerability and 11 bugs.

items.title: Ghost CMS SQL injection flaw exploited in large-scale ClickFix campaign
items.description: A large-scale campaign is exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-26980) in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code that triggers ClickFix attack flows. [...]

items.title: Laravel Lang packages hijacked to deploy credential-stealing malware
items.description: A supply chain attack targeting the Laravel Lang localization packages has exposed developers to a sophisticated credential-stealing malware campaign after attackers abused GitHub version tags to distribute malicious code through Composer packages. [...]

items.title: Italy disrupts CINEMAGOAL piracy app that stole streaming auth codes
items.description: Italian authorities have dismantled a piracy ecosystem centered around the CINEMAGOAL app that provided access to various streaming platforms, including Netflix, Disney+, and Spotify. [...]

items.title: An Example of Stack String in High Level Language, (Sat, May 23rd)
items.description: This week, I&#x27m attending the SEC670[1] training (â€œRed Teaming Tools - Developing Windows Implants, Shellcode, Command and Controlâ€). From my point of view, this training fits perfectly with FOR610 or FOR710 (malware analysis) because it addresses malware from the opposite: Instead of performing reverse engineering, you write malicious code! Always interesting to have another point of view.

items.title: CISA to allow researchers to report vulnerabilities to exploited bugs catalog
items.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) announced the creation of a nomination form on Thursday that they said enables “researchers, vendors, and industry partners” to report bugs that need to be added to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

items.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing-as-a-service after April Microsoft 365 attacks
items.description: The law enforcement agency published an advisory on Thursday about Kali365 — a Telegram-based service for cybercriminals that allows them to capture legitimate "OAuth" tokens enabling widespread access to Microsoft 365 environments.

items.title: Meta settles school district lawsuit claiming addictive design harmed students' mental health
items.description: The bellwether lawsuit was the first of at least 1,200 to be brought by a school district against Meta, Snap, YouTube and TikTok for similar alleged harms. The other cases have not yet been tried.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Akamai Joins Growing Chorus of Vendors Betting Big on Secure Enterprise Browsers
items.description: When Akamai announced its LayerX acquisition, the company joined a growing list of vendors adding secure enterprise browsers to their product portfolios.

items.title: Why the Supreme Court's Chatrie case could change the meaning of privacy in America
items.description: Lawyer Adam Unikowsky spoke with Recorded Future News about why he believes geofence searches are problematic and why the way the court rules could have a dramatic impact on Americans’ right to privacy.

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Healthcare Fends Off Increased Social Engineering Attacks
items.description: Ransomware and vendor breaches persist. The "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" (DBIR) highlights how evolving social engineering tactics make the sector more vulnerable.

items.title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
items.description: The advanced persistent threat group also relied on SOCKS proxies like SoftEther VPN, tunneling tools that act as a middleman between victim and attacker.

items.title: Cross-Platform NPM Stealer, (Fri, May 22nd)
items.description: I found a Node.js stealer that looked pretty well obfuscated. The file was not running out-of-the-box because it was uploaded on VT as â€œextracted-decoded.jsâ€ (and reformated). The SHA256 is 049300aa5dd774d6c984779a0570f59610399c71864b5d5c2605906db46ddeb9[1]. It did not run properly in a sandbox so only a static analysis was performed.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 22nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9942, (Fri, May 22nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
items.description: A security researcher discovered the API keys can still be used for up to 23 minutes after deletion, even though the cloud provider claims deletion is immediate.

items.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
items.description: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamics are very different than traditional IAM projects.

items.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
items.description: "Showboat" doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers.

items.title: Selective HTTP Proxying in Linux, (Thu, May 21st)
items.description: Recently, Rob wrote about a tool, Proxifier, that can intercept requests from specific processes. Proxifier is available for Windows, macOS, and Android. But I have not seen a generic Linux option yet. The advantage of a tool like Proxifier is the ability to target specific software. For debugging, reverse engineering, and similar tasks, selecting a specific process is quite useful, as it creates less noise to sift through and simplifies analysis.

items.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
items.description: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
items.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
items.description: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
